# Specification: Interactive Password Prompt for Stateless Install/Migrate Scripts

**Feature:** `stateless_password_prompt`  
**Date:** 2026-04-20  
**Status:** Ready for Implementation

---

## 1. Problem Definition

Both `scripts/stateless-setup.sh` (fresh install from live ISO) and
`scripts/migrate-to-stateless.sh` (in-place migration on an existing system)
currently hard-code the initial password for the `nimda` user as `"vexos"` in
`configuration-stateless.nix`. After the scripts finish they display a note
telling the user to manually edit that file.

This is a poor experience for two reasons:

1. The user must perform a manual, post-install step to set a non-default
   password, and many users skip it, leaving their systems with a
   world-known default credential.
2. Changing `configuration-stateless.nix` upstream would affect all users;
   there is no sanctioned per-machine override path today.

**Goal:** At install/migration time the user is asked for their desired
password. The value is hashed immediately in the shell script and written to a
machine-local NixOS override file (`stateless-user-override.nix`) that is
loaded by the flake only when present. The upstream `configuration-stateless.nix`
and `initialPassword = "vexos"` are left untouched; the override silences that
default at a higher priority. An empty prompt (Enter) keeps the default.

---

## 2. Affected Files

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `template/etc-nixos-flake.nix` | Give `mkStatelessVariant` its own builder; add optional `stateless-user-override.nix` loading |
| `scripts/stateless-setup.sh` | Add password prompt, hash, write override file before `git add`, copy to persist block |
| `scripts/migrate-to-stateless.sh` | Add password prompt, hash, write override file before `nixos-rebuild boot`, copy to persist block |

No changes to `configuration-stateless.nix`, `modules/impermanence.nix`, or
any host config. The upstream default password remains as a functional
fallback.

---

## 3. Template Flake Change — `template/etc-nixos-flake.nix`

### Current state

```nix
# Stateless role: minimal stack (no gaming/dev/virt/ASUS modules).
mkStatelessVariant = _mkVariantWith vexos-nix.nixosModules.statelessBase;
```

`_mkVariantWith` is a shared helper that accepts `baseModule`, `variant`, and
`gpuModule`. It has no role-specific optional file logic.

### Proposed change

Replace the one-liner partial application with an explicit builder modelled
directly on the existing `mkServerVariant` builder.

```nix
# Stateless role: minimal stack (no gaming/dev/virt/ASUS modules).
# Uses an explicit builder (rather than _mkVariantWith) so it can conditionally
# include a machine-local stateless-user-override.nix when present.
mkStatelessVariant = variant: gpuModule: nixpkgs.lib.nixosSystem {
  system = "x86_64-linux";
  modules =
    let
      modules = if builtins.isList gpuModule then gpuModule else [ gpuModule ];
      # Optional per-machine user override (password, etc.).
      # Generated by stateless-setup.sh / migrate-to-stateless.sh at install time.
      # When absent the upstream initialPassword = "vexos" default applies.
      userOverrideFile = ./stateless-user-override.nix;
      hasUserOverride  = builtins.pathExists userOverrideFile;
    in
    [
      { environment.etc."nixos/vexos-variant".text = "${variant}\n"; }
      bootloaderModule
      ./hardware-configuration.nix
      vexos-nix.nixosModules.statelessBase
    ]
    ++ modules
    ++ lib.optional hasUserOverride userOverrideFile;
};
```

**Why a dedicated builder rather than extending `_mkVariantWith`?**

- `_mkVariantWith` is shared by `mkVariant` (desktop) and `mkHtpcVariant`
  (HTPC), neither of which should load a stateless-specific override file.
- Mirroring the `mkServerVariant` pattern keeps the template internally
  consistent — each role that needs role-specific optional files has its own
  explicit builder.
- The change is additive and does not alter existing desktop, HTPC, or server
  behavior.

---

## 4. Override File Format — `stateless-user-override.nix`

The file is written at install/migration time to `/etc/nixos/`
(or `/mnt/etc/nixos/` during a fresh install). It must:

1. Clear `initialPassword` (set by the upstream config) via `lib.mkForce null`
   to satisfy the NixOS module assertion that prevents both options being set
   simultaneously.
2. Set `initialHashedPassword` to the SHA-512 crypt hash produced in the shell
   script.

### Hashed password variant (primary path)

```nix
# /etc/nixos/stateless-user-override.nix
# Generated by vexos stateless setup — do not edit manually.
# To regenerate: re-run scripts/stateless-setup.sh or scripts/migrate-to-stateless.sh.
{ lib, ... }:
{
  users.users.nimda = {
    initialPassword       = lib.mkForce null;
    initialHashedPassword = "<SHA-512-CRYPT-HASH>";
  };
}
```

### Plaintext fallback variant (only if `openssl` is unavailable)

```nix
# /etc/nixos/stateless-user-override.nix
# Generated by vexos stateless setup — PLAINTEXT FALLBACK (openssl not found).
{ lib, ... }:
{
  users.users.nimda.initialPassword = lib.mkForce "<plain-text-password>";
}
```

The plaintext fallback is included for completeness but is expected to never
trigger in practice (see §5).

**NixOS module assertion note:** `users.users.<name>.initialPassword` and
`users.users.<name>.initialHashedPassword` are both `nullOr str` options.
NixOS asserts that at most one is non-null per user. Setting
`initialPassword = lib.mkForce null` in the override file uses the Nix module
system's priority mechanism to win over the `initialPassword = "vexos"`
declaration in `configuration-stateless.nix` without conflict.

---

## 5. Password Hashing — Tool Selection

### Evaluation

| Tool | Available on NixOS live ISO? | Available on installed NixOS? | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `openssl passwd -6 -stdin` | **Yes** — `openssl` is in the ISO base system | **Yes** | SHA-512 crypt; `-stdin` avoids arg exposure |
| `mkpasswd -m sha-512` | **No** — requires `whois` package | Only if installed | Not safe to rely on |
| `python3 -c "import crypt; ..."` | Conditionally (Python 3 on ISO) | Python 3.13 removed `crypt` | Deprecated; not portable across NixOS 25.05+ |
| `nix-shell -p mkpasswd` | Requires network | Slower; interactive session issue | Impractical in installer flow |

**Decision: use `openssl passwd -6 -stdin` as primary.** Pipe the password
through stdin to prevent it appearing in process table (`ps` / `/proc`
arguments).

**Fallback:** If `openssl` is not found in `PATH`, write a plaintext
`initialPassword` override with a visible warning. This is safe enough for the
stateless role where `/` is an ephemeral tmpfs — a password persisted as
plaintext in the Nix store is equivalent in exposure to
`initialPassword = "vexos"` that already exists in the upstream config.

### Hash command

```bash
HASHED_PASSWORD=$(printf '%s' "$PASSWORD" | openssl passwd -6 -stdin)
```

`printf '%s'` is used instead of `echo` to avoid a trailing newline that would
become part of the hashed input on some shells.

---

## 6. Bash Password Prompt Implementation

The prompt block is inserted **after the GPU variant selection** and **before
the summary / confirmation block** in both scripts. Variable names and style
match the existing code in each script.

```bash
# ---------- Prompt: nimda user password -------------------------------------
echo ""
echo -e "${BOLD}Set a login password for the nimda user:${RESET}"
echo -e "${YELLOW}  Press Enter to keep the default password ('vexos').${RESET}"
echo -e "${YELLOW}  Note: the password resets to this value on every reboot (by design).${RESET}"
echo ""

NIMDA_HASHED_PASSWORD=""
NIMDA_PLAIN_PASSWORD=""

while true; do
  printf "Password (hidden, Enter = keep default): "
  read -rs NIMDA_PW </dev/tty
  echo ""

  # Empty input — keep default, no override file will be written
  if [ -z "$NIMDA_PW" ]; then
    echo -e "${YELLOW}  Keeping default password 'vexos'.${RESET}"
    break
  fi

  printf "Confirm password: "
  read -rs NIMDA_PW_CONFIRM </dev/tty
  echo ""

  if [ "$NIMDA_PW" != "$NIMDA_PW_CONFIRM" ]; then
    echo -e "${RED}Passwords do not match. Please try again.${RESET}"
    NIMDA_PW=""
    NIMDA_PW_CONFIRM=""
    continue
  fi

  # Hash with openssl SHA-512 crypt (-stdin prevents arg exposure in ps/proc)
  if command -v openssl &>/dev/null; then
    NIMDA_HASHED_PASSWORD=$(printf '%s' "$NIMDA_PW" | openssl passwd -6 -stdin)
    echo -e "${GREEN}  Password hashed successfully (SHA-512).${RESET}"
  else
    echo -e "${YELLOW}  Warning: openssl not found. Storing password as plaintext.${RESET}"
    NIMDA_PLAIN_PASSWORD="$NIMDA_PW"
  fi

  # Immediately clear the raw password variables from the environment
  NIMDA_PW=""
  NIMDA_PW_CONFIRM=""
  break
done
```

---

## 7. Override File Write — Per-Script Location

The override file is written **after `flake.nix` is in place** in each script
so it lands alongside the flake and can be referenced by `./stateless-user-override.nix`
in the flake's `builtins.pathExists` call.

### 7a. `scripts/stateless-setup.sh` (fresh install)

**Config root during install:** `/mnt/etc/nixos/`

Insert a new section immediately **after** the existing
`"Downloading vexos-nix template flake"` block and **before** the existing
`"Initialising git repo"` block. This ensures the file is present when
`git add .` stages all files.

```bash
# ---------- Write stateless user override (if password was set) -------------
if [ -n "$NIMDA_HASHED_PASSWORD" ]; then
  echo ""
  echo -e "${BOLD}Writing stateless user override (hashed password)...${RESET}"
  sudo tee /mnt/etc/nixos/stateless-user-override.nix > /dev/null << 'NIXEOF'
# /etc/nixos/stateless-user-override.nix
# Generated by vexos stateless setup — do not edit manually.
# To regenerate: re-run scripts/stateless-setup.sh or scripts/migrate-to-stateless.sh.
{ lib, ... }:
{
  users.users.nimda = {
    initialPassword       = lib.mkForce null;
    initialHashedPassword = "PLACEHOLDER_HASH";
  };
}
NIXEOF
  # Substitute the actual hash (avoids heredoc quoting issues with $ in hash)
  sudo sed -i "s|PLACEHOLDER_HASH|${NIMDA_HASHED_PASSWORD}|" \
    /mnt/etc/nixos/stateless-user-override.nix
  echo -e "${GREEN}✓ /mnt/etc/nixos/stateless-user-override.nix written.${RESET}"
elif [ -n "$NIMDA_PLAIN_PASSWORD" ]; then
  echo ""
  echo -e "${BOLD}Writing stateless user override (plaintext fallback)...${RESET}"
  sudo tee /mnt/etc/nixos/stateless-user-override.nix > /dev/null << 'NIXEOF'
# /etc/nixos/stateless-user-override.nix
# Generated by vexos stateless setup — PLAINTEXT FALLBACK.
{ lib, ... }:
{
  users.users.nimda.initialPassword = lib.mkForce "PLACEHOLDER_PW";
}
NIXEOF
  sudo sed -i "s|PLACEHOLDER_PW|${NIMDA_PLAIN_PASSWORD}|" \
    /mnt/etc/nixos/stateless-user-override.nix
  # Clear plaintext variable immediately after file write
  NIMDA_PLAIN_PASSWORD=""
  echo -e "${GREEN}✓ /mnt/etc/nixos/stateless-user-override.nix written (plaintext).${RESET}"
# else: no override file written; default 'vexos' password remains active
fi
```

In the existing **persist block** (`Persisting NixOS config files to /persistent`),
add one additional copy line:

```bash
sudo cp /mnt/etc/nixos/stateless-user-override.nix \
  /mnt/persistent/etc/nixos/ 2>/dev/null || true
```

In the **completion message**, replace the hard-coded credential lines:

```bash
if [ -n "$NIMDA_HASHED_PASSWORD" ] || [ -n "$NIMDA_PLAIN_PASSWORD" ]; then
  echo -e "  Password: ${CYAN}(the password you entered)${RESET}"
else
  echo -e "  Password: ${CYAN}vexos${RESET} ${YELLOW}(default — change by re-running this script or editing stateless-user-override.nix)${RESET}"
fi
```

Also remove the three `${YELLOW}Note:` lines about manually editing
`configuration-stateless.nix` and replace with a single updated note:

```bash
echo -e "${YELLOW}Note: The password resets to the value baked at install time on every reboot.${RESET}"
echo -e "${YELLOW}      To change it: re-run this script on the live ISO, or edit${RESET}"
echo -e "${YELLOW}      /etc/nixos/stateless-user-override.nix and run 'just rebuild'.${RESET}"
```

### 7b. `scripts/migrate-to-stateless.sh` (in-place migration)

**Config root on running system:** `/etc/nixos/`

Insert a new section immediately **after** the GPU variant selection block and
**before** the `nixos-rebuild boot` call.

```bash
# ---------- Write stateless user override (if password was set) -------------
if [ -n "$NIMDA_HASHED_PASSWORD" ]; then
  echo ""
  echo -e "${BOLD}Writing stateless user override (hashed password)...${RESET}"
  tee /etc/nixos/stateless-user-override.nix > /dev/null << 'NIXEOF'
# /etc/nixos/stateless-user-override.nix
# Generated by vexos stateless migration — do not edit manually.
# To regenerate: re-run scripts/migrate-to-stateless.sh.
{ lib, ... }:
{
  users.users.nimda = {
    initialPassword       = lib.mkForce null;
    initialHashedPassword = "PLACEHOLDER_HASH";
  };
}
NIXEOF
  sed -i "s|PLACEHOLDER_HASH|${NIMDA_HASHED_PASSWORD}|" \
    /etc/nixos/stateless-user-override.nix
  echo -e "${GREEN}✓ /etc/nixos/stateless-user-override.nix written.${RESET}"
elif [ -n "$NIMDA_PLAIN_PASSWORD" ]; then
  echo ""
  echo -e "${BOLD}Writing stateless user override (plaintext fallback)...${RESET}"
  tee /etc/nixos/stateless-user-override.nix > /dev/null << 'NIXEOF'
# /etc/nixos/stateless-user-override.nix
# Generated by vexos stateless migration — PLAINTEXT FALLBACK.
{ lib, ... }:
{
  users.users.nimda.initialPassword = lib.mkForce "PLACEHOLDER_PW";
}
NIXEOF
  sed -i "s|PLACEHOLDER_PW|${NIMDA_PLAIN_PASSWORD}|" \
    /etc/nixos/stateless-user-override.nix
  NIMDA_PLAIN_PASSWORD=""
  echo -e "${GREEN}✓ /etc/nixos/stateless-user-override.nix written (plaintext).${RESET}"
fi
```

In the **persist block** (`Persisting NixOS config files to @persist`), add:

```bash
cp /etc/nixos/stateless-user-override.nix \
  "${BTRFS_MOUNT}/@persist/etc/nixos/" 2>/dev/null && \
  echo -e "  ${GREEN}✓ stateless-user-override.nix persisted${RESET}" || true
```

In the **completion message**, apply the same credential output logic as §7a.

---

## 8. Persistence Design

Both scripts already have a "persist to @persist / /persistent" block that
copies `flake.nix`, `flake.lock`, and `hardware-configuration.nix`. The
`stateless-user-override.nix` file must be added to that block in both scripts.

**Why persistence is required:**  
After the first stateless boot, `/` is a tmpfs and `/etc/nixos` is bind-mounted
from `/persistent/etc/nixos` (as declared by `modules/impermanence.nix`). If
the override file is not in `@persist`, it will not exist at `/etc/nixos/` on
subsequent boots. The flake's `builtins.pathExists` would then evaluate `false`,
and `nixos-rebuild` or `just rebuild` would silently revert to the upstream
default password — the most dangerous silent regression case.

The copy uses `2>/dev/null || true` (matching existing script style) because if
the user chose the default password (empty input), no override file was written,
and the copy silently no-ops.

---

## 9. Summary and Confirmation Block Update

In `stateless-setup.sh`, the summary block currently shows:
```
  GPU variant: ${VARIANT}
```

Add a password line to the summary:
```bash
if [ -n "$NIMDA_HASHED_PASSWORD" ]; then
  echo "  Password:    (custom — SHA-512 hashed)"
elif [ -n "$NIMDA_PLAIN_PASSWORD" ]; then
  echo "  Password:    (custom — plaintext fallback)"
else
  echo "  Password:    vexos (default)"
fi
```

Apply the same addition to the summary block in `migrate-to-stateless.sh`.

---

## 10. Cleanup — `cleanup()` Trap in `stateless-setup.sh`

The existing `cleanup()` function removes `/tmp/disk-password`. No change is
needed here because we never write the password to a temp file; it is handled
entirely in shell variables and wiped after use. Add a comment confirming this:

```bash
cleanup() {
  rm -f /tmp/disk-password
  # NIMDA_PW and NIMDA_PW_CONFIRM are cleared inline after use;
  # no temp password files are written.
}
```

---

## 11. Security Considerations

| Risk | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| Password visible in `ps` output | Use `printf '%s' "$PW" \| openssl passwd -6 -stdin` (stdin not argv) |
| Raw password lingers in memory | Unset `NIMDA_PW` and `NIMDA_PW_CONFIRM` immediately after hashing |
| Plaintext password in Nix store | Only occurs in openssl fallback; functionally equivalent to existing `initialPassword = "vexos"` |
| Hash in store (SHA-512 crypt) | SHA-512 crypt ($6$) is the NixOS standard for `initialHashedPassword`; acceptable for an ephemeral default |
| `sed -i` PLACEHOLDER substitution with special chars in hash | SHA-512 crypt output is `[a-zA-Z0-9/.$]` — no sed metacharacters; safe for direct substitution |
| Override file world-readable | `/etc/nixos/` is root-owned 755; override file inherits standard umask; consistent with `hardware-configuration.nix` handling |
| PLACEHOLDER_HASH collision with legit hash content | The placeholder string `PLACEHOLDER_HASH` cannot appear in a SHA-512 crypt hash (which uses only the crypt-64 alphabet) |

---

## 12. Risks and Mitigations

| Risk | Likelihood | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|
| User sets a password then re-runs script on existing system — old override silently replaced | Low | Acceptable: re-running install/migrate intentionally replaces prior config |
| `builtins.pathExists` evaluated at flake eval time — override absent on live ISO if written after `git add` | **High if not careful** | Spec requires override file written BEFORE `git add .` in `stateless-setup.sh` |
| NixOS module assertion fires (both `initialPassword` and `initialHashedPassword` set) | Certain without fix | Resolved by `initialPassword = lib.mkForce null` in override file |
| Override file not persisted — reverts to default on first stateless boot | **High** — silent regression | Spec requires copy to `@persist` in both scripts' persist blocks |
| Password with `|` or `\n` breaks `sed -i` substitution | Low — `$6$` hashes don't contain `|` | SHA-512 crypt alphabet is `[./a-zA-Z0-9$]`; `|` never appears |
| `openssl passwd -6` not available (OpenSSL < 1.1.0) | Very low — NixOS 25.05 ships OpenSSL 3.x | Plaintext fallback covers this; warn user visibly |

---

## 13. Implementation Steps (for Implementation Subagent)

1. **`template/etc-nixos-flake.nix`**  
   Replace the `mkStatelessVariant = _mkVariantWith ...` one-liner with the
   explicit builder from §3.

2. **`scripts/stateless-setup.sh`**  
   a. Insert password prompt block (§6) after GPU variant prompt, before summary.  
   b. Add password line to summary block (§9).  
   c. Insert override file write block (§7a) after flake download, before `git init`.  
   d. Add override file copy to persist block (§7a).  
   e. Update completion message credential lines (§7a).  
   f. Update `cleanup()` comment (§10).

3. **`scripts/migrate-to-stateless.sh`**  
   a. Insert password prompt block (§6) after GPU variant prompt, before `nixos-rebuild boot`.  
   b. Add password line to summary block (§9).  
   c. Insert override file write block (§7b) after GPU variant prompt, before rebuild.  
   d. Add override file copy to persist block (§7b).  
   e. Update completion message credential lines (§7b).

**Order constraint for `stateless-setup.sh`:** The override file must be
written AFTER `sudo curl ... -o /mnt/etc/nixos/flake.nix` and BEFORE
`sudo git -C /mnt/etc/nixos add .` so that `git add` stages it along with
`flake.nix` and the flake's `builtins.pathExists` can see it through the
`git+file:` flake reference used by `nixos-install`.

**LF line endings:** Per repo convention (`.gitattributes` enforces LF for
`*.sh`), all script edits must use LF-only line endings.
